title: 10 medicinal constituents of Sambucus ebulus (dwarf elder)

constituents_list.constituent_desc: Sambucus ebulus flavonoids is a group of bioactive compounds that are abundant in its leaves and stems. These flavonoids have been extensively studied for their potential health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. They have been shown to exhibit potent inhibitory effects on various enzymes, as well as protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals.

Sambucus ebulus quercetin is a type of flavonoid found in its berries. It has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including allergies, inflammation, and cancer. Quercetin's antioxidant properties help to reduce oxidative stress and protect against cell damage. Its anti-inflammatory effects have also been shown to alleviate symptoms of respiratory diseases such as bronchitis and asthma.

Sambucus ebulus kaempferol is a type of flavonoid compound that is abundant in the plant. Kaempferol has been extensively studied for its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may contribute to its potential health benefits. It has been shown to have protective effects against oxidative stress, cell damage, and inflammation, making it a promising candidate for the prevention or treatment of various diseases, including cardiovascular disease, cancer, and neurodegenerative disorders.

Sambucus ebulus isoquercetin is a bioflavonoid compound extracted from its fruit and leaves. It has been found to possess various pharmacological activities, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Isoquercetin has been shown to exhibit protective effects against oxidative stress, cardiovascular disease, and cancer, as well as having potential therapeutic benefits for respiratory and gastrointestinal disorders.

Sambucus ebulus rutin is a bioflavonoid compound extracted from the bark and leaves of the European elder tree. It has been used traditionally in folk medicine for its anticoagulant and anti-inflammatory properties, and has been shown to have potential health benefits including improving blood vessel health, reducing swelling, and alleviating symptoms of hemorrhoids and varicose veins.

Sambucus ebulus catechins is a type of polyphenol compound found in its berries and leaves. These catechins have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, making them potentially beneficial for human health. Research suggests that they may help protect against chronic diseases such as cardiovascular disease, cancer, and neurodegenerative disorders. The unique composition of Sambucus ebulus catechins also offers promising opportunities for the development of novel pharmaceuticals and food supplements.

Sambucus ebulus epicatechin is a bioactive compound found in the leaves and stems of the plant. It has been studied for its potential health benefits, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Epicatechin has been shown to have effects on cardiovascular health, such as lowering blood pressure and improving blood vessel function.

Sambucus ebulus chlorogenic acid is a bioactive compound found in its leaves and fruits. It has been extensively studied for its potential health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Chlorogenic acid has been shown to exhibit cardioprotective effects by reducing blood pressure, improving glucose metabolism, and inhibiting platelet aggregation. Its anti-cancer properties have also been investigated, with evidence suggesting it may help prevent cell proliferation and induce apoptosis in cancer cells.

Sambucus ebulus ellagic acid is a polyphenolic compound found in its berries and leaves. It has been extensively researched for its potential health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Ellagic acid has been shown to exhibit inhibitory effects on cancer cell growth and may help protect against cardiovascular disease, diabetes, and neurodegenerative disorders. Its bioactive compounds have also been used in traditional medicine for centuries, particularly in Asian cultures.

Sambucus ebulus gallic acid is a bioactive compound isolated from its fruits. It has been extensively studied for its antimicrobial, antiviral, and antioxidant properties, making it a potential natural remedy for various health issues. Gallic acid has also been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and anticancer activities, suggesting its therapeutic potential in treating chronic diseases. Its unique chemical structure makes it an attractive target for the development of novel pharmaceuticals and nutraceuticals.

intro: Sambucus ebulus has active constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins. These compounds contribute to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The flavonoids and phenolic acids have been shown to alleviate symptoms of allergies, such as itching and inflammation, while the saponins may help improve cardiovascular health by reducing cholesterol levels and blood pressure. Overall, these constituents can help improve people's lives by providing relief from chronic conditions like hay fever and potentially lowering the risk of heart disease.
intro_desc: Sambucus ebulus has active constituents such as flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids. These constituents possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can alleviate symptoms of allergies and infections. Flavonoids, like quercetin, can reduce inflammation and alleviate respiratory issues, improving quality of life for individuals with conditions such as asthma. Alkaloids, like sambunigrin, exhibit antimicrobial activity, fighting off infections and promoting overall health.
